prompt: `LFPSelection` has a `target_sampling_rate` field. What does this control, and what goes wrong if I set it too low for a ripple-band analysis I want to do later?
behavioral_checks: Explains that target_sampling_rate sets the downsampled output rate of LFPV1
Connects the rate to the Nyquist constraint for downstream band filters (e.g., ripple band 150–250 Hz needs > 500 Hz sampling)
Names 1000 Hz as the canonical default and the consequence of going lower (aliasing in the downstream band)
Does not say the sample rate is arbitrary or that any value works (this would contradict the Nyquist constraint)
